root_text.an8.71:1.1: “Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti, no ca sīlavā.
root_text.an8.71:1.5: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti sīlavā ca,
root_text.an8.71:2.1: Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti sīlavā ca, no ca bahussuto.
root_text.an8.71:2.4: ‘kintāhaṁ saddho ca assaṁ, sīlavā ca, bahussuto cā’ti.
root_text.an8.71:2.5: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti sīlavā ca bahussuto ca,
root_text.an8.71:3.1: Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti sīlavā ca bahussuto ca, no ca dhammakathiko …pe…
root_text.an8.71:3.3: parisāvacaro ca, no ca visārado parisāya dhammaṁ deseti …pe…
root_text.an8.71:3.4: visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, no ca catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī …pe…
root_text.an8.71:3.5: catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, no ca āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati;
root_text.an8.71:3.8: ‘kintāhaṁ saddho ca assaṁ, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseyyaṁ, catunnañca jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī assaṁ akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an8.71:4.1: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, catunnañca jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati;
root_text.an8.71:4.3: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu samantapāsādiko ca hoti sabbākāraparipūro cā”ti.

translation_text.an8.71:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant is faithful but not ethical.
translation_text.an8.71:1.2: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an8.71:1.4: ‘How can I become faithful and ethical?’
translation_text.an8.71:1.5: When the mendicant is faithful and ethical,
translation_text.an8.71:2.1: A mendicant is faithful and ethical, but not learned.
translation_text.an8.71:2.2: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an8.71:2.4: ‘How can I become faithful, ethical, and learned?’
translation_text.an8.71:2.5: When the mendicant is faithful, ethical, and learned,
translation_text.an8.71:3.1: A mendicant is faithful, ethical, and learned, but not a Dhamma speaker. …
translation_text.an8.71:3.3: they don’t teach Dhamma to the assembly with assurance …
translation_text.an8.71:3.4: they don’t get the four absorptions—blissful meditations in this life that belong to the higher mind—when they want, without trouble or difficulty …
translation_text.an8.71:3.5: they don’t realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an8.71:3.6: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an8.71:3.8: ‘How can I become faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, one who gets the four absorptions when they want, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements?’
translation_text.an8.71:4.1: When they’re faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, one who gets the four absorptions when they want, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements,
translation_text.an8.71:4.3: A mendicant who possesses these eight qualities is impressive all around, and is complete in every respect.”
